comment: Premise-level source/provenance audit. Theorem 3 bridge-certificate assumptions were removed from the paper interface and replaced by closed source-shaped wrappers.

assumption_positive_recommendation_utili.reason: The source model and Theorem 3 opposing-type construction use strictly positive recommendation utilities.
assumption_positive_recommendation_utili.source_location: Model/Theorem 3 setup, cached source text lines 368-370 and 2080-2083; Appendix C Lemma 1 lines 1740-1746

hPos : S.model.Positive.reason: Appendix C Lemma 1 states the strictly positive utility premise w_ij > 0 for all items.

hPos : R.data.model.Positive.reason: Theorem 3's opposing-type model states v1 > v2 > ... > vn > 0, so the corresponding original recommendation model has positive utilities.

hPos' : R'.data.model.Positive.reason: The comparison model in Theorem 3 has the same positive value vector v with a different population share alpha.

assumption_theorem3_opposing_type_model.reason: Theorem 3 is stated for two opposing user types with a common strictly decreasing positive value vector.

hred : R.reduced = OpposingTypes.twoType.reason: The source defines the two-type model by the values v_j and v_{n-j+1}; the reduction witness equality pins the Lean model to that source construction.

hred' : R'.reduced = OpposingTypes.twoTy.reason: The comparison instance uses the same opposing-type construction and value vector at population share alpha'.

hpos : ∀ j : Item n, 0 < v j.reason: The source assumes v1 > v2 > ... > vn > 0.

hdec : OpposingTypes.StrictlyDecreasingB.reason: The strict ordering v1 > v2 > ... > vn is the Lean strict-decreasing-by-index condition.

assumption_theorem3_first_half_alpha_dom.reason: Theorem 3 first-half monotonicity is stated for 0 < alpha <= alpha' <= 1/2.

halpha0 : 0 < alpha.reason: The source states the first-half interval as 0 < alpha <= 1/2.

halpha1 : alpha < 1.reason: The theorem domain is alpha in (0,1), split around 1/2.

halpha0' : 0 < alpha'.reason: The comparison alpha' is in the same first-half source interval.

halpha1' : alpha' < 1.reason: The theorem domain is alpha' in (0,1), split around 1/2.

halpha_le : alpha ≤ alpha'.reason: The Lean comparison expresses monotonicity in alpha over the first half.

halpha_half' : alpha' ≤ 1 / 2.reason: The comparison endpoint alpha' remains in the first-half interval.

assumption_theorem3_second_half_alpha_do.reason: Theorem 3 second-half monotonicity is stated for 1/2 <= alpha <= alpha' < 1.

halpha0 : 0 < alpha.reason: The theorem domain is alpha in (0,1), split around 1/2.

halpha0' : 0 < alpha'.reason: The comparison alpha' is in the same source domain.

halpha1' : alpha' < 1.reason: The theorem states the second-half interval up to alpha < 1.

halpha_le : alpha ≤ alpha'.reason: The Lean comparison expresses monotonicity in alpha over the second half.

halpha_half' : 1 / 2 ≤ alpha'.reason: The comparison endpoint alpha' remains in the second-half interval.

assumption_theorem4_at_least_three_items.reason: The Theorem 4 construction uses a nontrivial item geometry with more than two items.
assumption_theorem4_at_least_three_items.source_location: Theorem 4 construction and Appendix E proof, cached source text lines 456-470 and 3338-3368

hn : 2 < n.reason: The source construction uses beta > 1/n and the three-type estimated matrix; the Lean wrapper records the nontrivial n > 2 item condition used by the construction.

assumption_theorem4_true_model_reduction.reason: The Theorem 4 wrapper identifies the true model with the paper's two-opposing-type source construction.
assumption_theorem4_true_model_reduction.source_location: Theorem 4 construction, cached source text lines 456-470 and appendix lines 3338-3368

htrue : E.trueModel = Rtrue.data.model.reason: The theorem compares the true utility matrix w with the estimated matrix w-hat; the Lean premise connects the true model field to the two-type reduction witness representing w.

assumption_theorem4_estimated_model_redu.reason: The Theorem 4 wrapper identifies the estimated model with the paper's three-type estimated utility construction.
assumption_theorem4_estimated_model_redu.source_location: Theorem 4 construction, cached source text lines 456-470 and appendix lines 3338-3369

hestimated : E.estimatedModel = Rest.dat.reason: The theorem's w-hat construction is represented by the three-type estimated reduction witness in Lean.

assumption_theorem4_displayed_reduced_mo.reason: Theorem 4 uses the displayed true and estimated reduced matrices.

hredTrue : Rtrue.reduced = OpposingTypes.reason: The no-misestimation true model uses the equal-proportion opposing two-type construction; Lean pins it to the displayed small-value vector used to make the misestimation price large.

hredEst : Rest.reduced = OpposingTypes.t.reason: The estimated model is the paper's three-type w-hat construction with the beta cold-start share and the small-value vector.

assumption_theorem4_cold_start_type_wiri.reason: Theorem 4's cold-start users are estimated as the third type while their true row is one of the two opposing rows.

hknown0 : ∀ u : User m, Rest.data.types..reason: The estimated known first type corresponds to the true first opposing row.

hknown1 : ∀ u : User m, Rest.data.types..reason: The estimated known second type corresponds to the true second opposing row.

htrueType : Rtrue.data.types.toType u =.reason: One Theorem 4 wrapper handles a mis-estimated user whose true row is the first opposing type.
The second Theorem 4 wrapper handles a mis-estimated user whose true row is the second opposing type.

hestimatedType : Rest.data.types.toType.reason: The paper denotes the third estimated type z as the mis-estimated users.

assumption_theorem4_parameter_domain.reason: Theorem 4's arbitrary-large misestimation conclusion is parameterized by epsilon and beta.

heps : 0 < eps.reason: The source states the large-misestimation bullet for arbitrary epsilon and chooses values to make the price exceed 1 - epsilon.

hbeta_half : beta < 1 / 2.reason: The Lean construction fixes the cold-start third type as a minority estimated type; beta < 1/2 is the formal domain used by this two-known-types plus cold-start construction.
